pattern: Two pointers advancing through the already-sorted lists compare overlapping endpoints to emit each intersection in order, discarding the interval that ends earlier.
hints: Both lists are sorted and disjoint, so you never need to look backward.
For the current interval from each list, their overlap (if any) is [max of starts, min of ends].
Walk a pointer in each list: the intersection is valid when max(starts) <= min(ends); always advance the pointer whose interval has the smaller end, since it cannot intersect anything further.

edge_inputs.description: interleaved intervals with point and span overlaps
touching only at a single shared endpoint
fully disjoint intervals produce no overlap
one interval contains several from the other

stress.description: two random disjoint sorted interval lists
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    def build(n):
        out = []
        cur = rng.randint(0, 5)
        for _ in range(n):
            s = cur + rng.randint(1, 5)
            e = s + rng.randint(0, 6)
            out.append([s, e])
            cur = e
        return out
    n = max(0, min(size, 500))
    return [build(n), build(n)]
stress.note: each list strictly sorted and pairwise disjoint by construction
